title: tkform project file
description: Project file for the tkform VS Code extension. Describes a Tkinter GUI (canvas, widgets, events, variables, menus, resources). This schema covers structural shape only; per-widget prop allow-lists, cross-references, duplicate-id checks, Python code compilation, and layout mixing are enforced by the engine validator (run `scripts/validate_project.py`).

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. The designer always normalizes loaded files to 2; write 2 in new files. (Files written as 1 are still accepted but will be migrated on save.)

name.description: Project / window name. Used as the export filename base.

canvasWidth.description: Initial Tkinter root window width in pixels. Also the design canvas width.

canvasHeight.description: Initial Tkinter root window height in pixels. Also the design canvas height.

widgets.description: Widget instances. Each becomes a Tkinter widget in the generated Python. See references/widgets.md for the per-type prop allow-list.

tkTheme.description: ttk theme name. Common values: 'default', 'clam', 'alt', 'classic'. Falls back to 'default' if the runtime lacks the requested theme.

menuBar.description: Top-level menu bar. null/omitted = no menu bar. See references/menus.md.

rootBg.description: Root window background color (e.g. '#ffffff'). Empty string = Tkinter default.

rootResizable.description: Whether the root window can be resized by the user.

variables.description: Tk variable declarations (StringVar/IntVar/DoubleVar/BooleanVar). Widgets reference these by name via props.variable / props.textvariable. See references/variables.md.

nonVisuals.description: Non-visual components: Timer, FileDialog, ColorChooser, MessageBox. See references/non-visuals.md.

resources.description: Image resources embedded as base64 data URLs. Widgets reference these by id via props.image. See references/resources.md.

pythonIdentifier.description: Must be a valid Python identifier (not a reserved keyword). The engine rejects keywords like 'class', 'for', etc.

id.description: Stable unique id within the project. Used as parentId target and bindings target. Convention: '<type>-<slug>' e.g. 'frame-login', 'button-save'.

type.description: Widget type. Maps directly to a Tkinter widget class. ttk widgets (Notebook, Progressbar, Combobox, Treeview, Sizegrip, Separator, TtkPanedWindow) cannot use bg/fg/padx/pady/etc. See references/widgets.md.

name.description: Becomes the Python attribute name on the generated UI object. Must be unique across widgets.

parentId.description: Parent widget id, or null for direct children of the root window. The parent MUST be a container type (Frame, LabelFrame, Canvas, PanedWindow, TtkPanedWindow, Notebook, Toplevel). See references/layout.md.

x.description: X position (place layout) or design hint (grid layout).

y.description: Y position (place layout) or design hint (grid layout).

props.description: Tkinter widget options. The allowed keys depend on `type`. IMPORTANT: a `command` key here must be a Python identifier reference (e.g. 'self.on_click'); for inline code use `events.command` instead. See references/widgets.md for the per-type allow-list.

locked.description: Designer-only: locks the widget against accidental drag in the canvas. Has no effect on generated code.

activeTab.description: Designer-only: which Notebook tab is selected in the editor.

xscrollcommand.description: Widget id whose xscrollcommand this widget drives (typically a Scrollbar).

yscrollcommand.description: Widget id whose yscrollcommand this widget drives (typically a Scrollbar).

command.description: For Scrollbar: target widget id being scrolled. The target must be a scrollable type (Text, Listbox, Entry, Treeview).

events.description: Event handlers. Two forms are both accepted. MAP form: { 'command': 'print(1)' } or { '<Leave>': { handlerName, code } }. ARRAY form: [{ id, event, kind, handlerName, code, enabled }]. See references/events.md.

layoutManager.description: Per-widget layout manager. Children of the SAME parent must all use the same manager, EXCEPT children of Toplevel, PanedWindow, TtkPanedWindow. Omitting implies 'place'. See references/layout.md.

event.description: Tk event name. 'command' (button/selection command) or a Tk bind sequence such as '<Leave>', '<Button-1>', '<Key>'.

handlerName.description: Generated Python method name. If omitted, an anonymous handler is generated. Must be unique across ALL enabled handlers in the project.

code.description: Python code body. Compiled with `def <handlerName>(event=None): <code>`; must be syntactically valid. May reference Tk variables by name (e.g. 'filter_var.get()').

enabled.description: If false, the handler is skipped at codegen time.

name.description: Python attribute name. Widgets reference this via props.variable / props.textvariable.

defaultValue.description: Initial value as a string. The engine casts to the var type. For BooleanVar use '1'/'0' or 'True'/'False'.

type.description: See references/non-visuals.md. FileDialog has a special `filetypes` prop parsing rule.

events.description: Same shape as widget events. Timer typically binds 'command' (tick). See references/events.md.

name.description: Display name; converted to a Python identifier for the resource attribute. Avoid spaces/symbols.

dataUrl.description: Base64 data URL ('data:image/png;base64,...'). Decoded payload must be <= 5MB and valid base64.

label.description: Menu label as shown on the menu bar (e.g. 'File', 'Edit').

items.description: Items in display order. Cascade items contain nested `children`.

label.description: Display label. Required for non-separator items.

kind.description: Item behavior. 'cascade' items have non-empty `children`. 'separator' renders a horizontal rule. Defaults to 'command'.

accelerator.description: Display-only hint shown on the right (e.g. 'Ctrl+R').

acceleratorBinding.description: Tk bind sequence that actually triggers the item (e.g. '<Control-r>'). Bound on root.

variable.description: For checkbutton/radiobutton: the Tk variable to bind. Must be declared in `variables`.

value.description: For radiobutton: the value this choice writes to `variable`.

events.description: Handlers. For 'command' items the 'command' event fires on click.

children.description: Submenu items (only meaningful when kind='cascade'). Subject to the same max nesting depth (64) as widgets.
